approval_notes: The originating session approved the 6-slice F004 plan and instructed the Chief to build Slice 1 only (safe fetch guard + adapter contract) in interactive mode, then STOP: do not merge, do not start Slice 2, do not flip any passes flag.

This is the FIRST of six F004 slices. It is the security spine that unblocks every other slice: it is stdlib-only (no dependency approval needed), adds no migration, and directly attacks the highest-risk acceptance concern (step 2 network safety).
F004 stays passes:false after this slice. Flipping F004 passes:true requires a separate fresh-context FULL-EPIC Level 2 evaluation after all slices land, exactly as F002/F003 were closed out.
No new runtime dependency: the transport and contract are pure standard-library Python (urllib/http.client, ipaddress, socket, ssl, hashlib, json). The requirements-sync guard must stay green.
objective: Introduce the ingestion security spine and adapter contract: a single hardened, injectable fetch/transport layer that is the only code permitted to touch the network (disabled by default), plus the SourceAdapter ABC that hides all provider-specific logic behind the fixed seven-method contract (discover/fetch/canonicalize/extract/validate/evidence/health). Adapters depend only on the Fetcher seam, emit candidate facts in a pre-publication state only, and produce evidence solely for official sources, so there is no adapter-level path to a verified or published offer.
scope: fetch.py: pure, independently-testable policy functions (screen_url scheme+host allowlist, host_matches_allowlist exact-or-subdomain, is_blocked_address SSRF/private-range/metadata via ipaddress with ipv4-mapped unwrap, validate_content_type MIME prefix match) plus a FetchPolicy carrier and a FetchResult carrier (final_url, status, mime, body bytes, content_hash sha256, fetched_at, redirect_chain). OfflineFetcher default that never opens a socket; SafeFetcher live transport gated behind enable_network=False by default, with an injectable resolver, manual redirect handling that re-screens allowlist+SSRF on every hop, connect/read timeouts, and a streamed max-size cap with early abort. A typed FetchError hierarchy naming each rejection reason.

base.py: SourceAdapter abc.ABC enforcing the seven contract methods, plus frozen typed carriers SourceDocument, CandidateFacts (validates trust_level and pre-publication verification_state in __post_init__), EvidenceLocation, AdapterHealth; a guard_evidence helper that raises if a non-official source tries to emit evidence.
vocab.py: closed vocabularies VERIFICATION_STATES, PRE_PUBLICATION_STATES, TRUST_LEVELS (+ TRUST_OFFICIAL and is_official helper) for the in-flight ingestion lifecycle.
reference.py: one minimal reference structured/JSON adapter (ReferenceJSONAdapter) making the contract concrete; missing fields degrade to warnings, malformed JSON is rejected (never guessed).
FixtureFetcher test transport (deterministic, offline) that still enforces scheme+host+MIME+size screening; FixtureResponse carrier.
tests/unit/test_ingest_fetch.py and tests/unit/test_ingest_contract.py covering every Slice 1 acceptance criterion offline.
out_of_scope: Alembic migration 0004, candidate/discovery_candidate tables, ScanRun orchestration, and any database persistence (F004 slice 2).
RSS, HTML, structured-API, and MCP provider adapters (slices 4-5).
Community-discovery importer and the definitive community-vs-official separation hardening + epic close-out (slice 6).
Enabling real network egress by default, or any live scan against a real provider.
Creating any Offer/OfferVersion or publishing anything.
Marking F004 (or any feature) passes:true; adding managed-cloud services or heavyweight dependencies; advancing past this slice.
acceptance_criteria: A non-allowlisted host is rejected before any connection (DisallowedHostError) and the injected resolver is never called for it.
A redirect to a disallowed or private/loopback host is rejected mid-chain (the target is re-screened on every hop).
A non-https URL is rejected (DisallowedSchemeError) under the default scheme allowlist.

Private, loopback, link-local, ULA, reserved, and the cloud metadata address (169.254.169.254) are rejected by is_blocked_address, including ipv4-mapped IPv6 forms; DNS-rebinding-style resolution to a private address is rejected by SafeFetcher via the injected resolver.
A slow response aborts within the configured timeout budget (FetchTimeoutError) against an explicitly-allowlisted 127.0.0.1 loopback server.
An oversize response is aborted once it exceeds max_bytes (ResponseTooLargeError), without buffering the whole body.
A disallowed MIME type is rejected (DisallowedContentTypeError).
The default fetcher (OfflineFetcher) raises NetworkDisabledError and opens no socket even when socket.socket is monkeypatched to fail; SafeFetcher with enable_network=False also refuses without I/O.
The SourceAdapter ABC cannot be instantiated when a subclass omits any of the seven contract methods (TypeError).

The reference adapter drives all seven methods end to end against a FixtureFetcher + JSON fixture, producing candidate facts in a pre-publication state and evidence for an official source; a community/unknown source produces zero evidence (guard_evidence raises if attempted); malformed JSON is handled as rejected with a warning, never guessed.

Regression green: scripts/test.ps1 and scripts/check.ps1 -NodeAudit pass offline (ruff, format, pytest, prettier, eslint, detect-secrets, pip-audit, npm audit), including the requirements-sync guard (no new dependency).
No unsupported free-tier/zero-cost/Z0 product claim is introduced; this slice adds transport + contract code only and asserts no service is free.

A fresh-context independent Level 2 evaluator records an adversarial verdict in agent-state/evaluation.json before any status change is claimed; F004 remains passes:false (epic close-out is a separate full-epic evaluation).

end_to_end_steps: Run tests/unit/test_ingest_fetch.py and tests/unit/test_ingest_contract.py and confirm every Slice 1 acceptance criterion passes offline.
Run scripts/test.ps1 and scripts/check.ps1 -NodeAudit and confirm both pass end-to-end (no new dependency; requirements-sync green).
Confirm detect-secrets is green and no credential appears in code or output.
negative_tests: Non-allowlisted host => DisallowedHostError before connect; resolver not called.
Redirect to private/loopback/disallowed host => rejected mid-chain.
Slow loopback server => FetchTimeoutError within budget.
OfflineFetcher.fetch with socket.socket patched to raise => still NetworkDisabledError (no socket opened).
Adapter subclass missing evidence() => TypeError on instantiation.
Community-source adapter attempting evidence => PermissionError (no evidence path).

risks.data_migration: None. This slice adds no schema and no migration; persistence is slice 2.
risks.security_privacy: This slice IS the security boundary. Live egress is disabled by default; all tests are offline against fixtures or an explicitly-allowlisted loopback server. No credentials are read or stored; detect-secrets stays green.
risks.portability: Pure standard-library Python; no managed-cloud dependency, no Redis; PostgreSQL (unused by this slice) remains the sole mandatory stateful service. Runs identically on amd64 and arm64.
risks.zero_cost_safety: Adapters emit candidate facts in a pre-publication state only and produce evidence for official sources exclusively; there is no adapter-level path to a verified or published offer, preserving the no-false-claim guarantee.
risks.regression: No dependency change, so the requirements-sync guard is unaffected. The package is additive and imported by nothing at runtime yet, so existing services and tests are unchanged.
risks.supply_chain: No new dependency (Python or npm) is added by this slice.

notes: The originating session approved the F004 plan and scoped this as Slice 1 only, to be built in interactive mode and then handed back for review + a Level 2 slice evaluation before any further slice. This slice does not mark F004 passing and adds no dependency and no migration.
